title: Glockeria haematodes
taxonomy: Glockeria haematodes is classified under the family Gesneriaceae, which consists of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. This family comprises approximately 130 genera and 3,000 species. Glockeria haematodes, specifically, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the order Lamiales. The order Lamiales is a group of flowering plants that also includes mint, honeysuckle, and snapdragons. Glockeria haematodes is native to tropical Africa.
uses: Glockeria haematodes is a plant species found in various parts of the world, but information on its medicinal uses is limited. However, some studies suggest that the plant may have antimalarial properties, although more research is needed to confirm this.
